calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Mtn For Writ Of Mandamus Pursuant To Code Of Covil Procedure 1094.5 And Administrative Record
Off Calendar Oct-15-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER STEPHEN SMITH'S MOTION FOR WRIT OF MANDAMUS PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 1094.5 AND ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD. Appearances: Michael S. Hebel of the SF Police Officers Association (861.0211) for petitioner and moving party; Rebecca Liu of the Office of the City Attorney (554.3870) for respondent and opposing party. The court made a personal disclosure and no objections having been raised, the matter was argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied. The court has used its independent judgment and reviewed the entire record, and finds the decision of the hearing officer is based on substantial evidence and supported by the weight of the evidence. Dr. Leonard's findings that petitioner has no disability and no preclusions based upon a review of the medical records, physical examinations, and review of a sub rosa videotape are far more persuasive then Dr. Blau's recommendation for work restrictions on a prophylactic basis only. The errors in Dr. Leonard's report do not pertain to the cardiac issue and do not discredit his medical opinion that petitioner is not incapacitated from performing his duties. The medical records and test results weigh heavily in support of the hearing officer's decision. Order signed in open court. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Reporter: Candace Yount, CSR #2737

Ntc Of Mtn For Writ Of Mandamus Pursuant To Code Of Covil Procedure 1094.5 And Administrative Record
Off Calendar Oct-04-2010 Continued To Oct-15-10 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
PETITIONER STEPHEN SMITH'S MOTION FOR WRIT OF MANDAMUS PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 1094.5.

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - continued on the court's own motion to October 15, 2010 for completion of the administrative record. By October 7, 2010, respondent must provide to the court the videotape from Interquest that was reviewed at the administrative hearing. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
